name: Barranco de las Sabinas to Guazalamanco Trail
name_original: Cascada del Guazalamanco - circular trailrunning por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: Jog the Barranco de las Sabinas to Guazalamanco Trail, a difficult 6.9-mile route with waterfalls and varied terrain in Cazorla Natural Park
region_intro: Jogging routes Pozo Alcón are situated at the entrance to the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, offering a diverse natural environment. The region features lush mountains, dense forests, and the tranquil waters of the Embalse de La Bolera reservoir. Runners can explore varied terrain, from riverside paths along the Guadalentín River to trails through unique natural features like the "Enchanted Forest." This landscape provides options for different running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Pozo Alcón The most popular jogging route is Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This route leads through a unique forest with centuries-old fig trees forming a dense canopy. Another top favourite among local runners is Barranco de las Sabinas to Guazalamanco Trail, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This trail offers a challenging run through diverse ecosystems and mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco, a 1.9 miles (3.0 km) trail leading through the Guazalamanco valley to its waterfall, often completed in about 23 minutes. Jogging around Pozo Alcón is defined by the landscapes of the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park, the shores of the Embalse de La Bolera, and the Guadalentín River valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Pozo Alcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Pozo Alcón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;7&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated jogging routes around Pozo Alcón listed in this guide. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy loops to more challenging mountain runs, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Pozo Alcón?
A: The mild and pleasant weather during &lt;em&gt;spring (April to June)&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;fall (September to November)&lt;/em&gt; makes these seasons ideal for outdoor activities, including running, in Pozo Alcón. The cooler temperatures and vibrant natural scenery enhance the experience.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, Pozo Alcón offers routes suitable for all levels. For an easy jog, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.9 miles (3.0 km) long and takes about 23 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature waterfalls in Pozo Alcón?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the Guazalamanco waterfall. Additionally, the region boasts the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua (Tíscar)&lt;/a&gt;, which features an internal waterfall and is often accessible via trails.

Q: Are there any scenic running paths around the Embalse de La Bolera reservoir?
A: Yes, the Embalse de La Bolera is a prominent natural feature in the region, offering impressive panoramic views and a serene environment. You can find beautiful jogging paths along its shores, providing a tranquil setting for your run.

Q: What unique natural attractions can I discover while jogging in Pozo Alcón?
A: Pozo Alcón is home to several unique natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989183384/enchanted-forest-of-las-higueras-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes you through a magical forest with centuries-old fig trees forming a dense canopy, crystalline waterfalls, and turquoise pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua (Tíscar)&lt;/a&gt; is another natural wonder with unique rock formations.

Q: Are there viewpoints along the running trails in Pozo Alcón?
A: Yes, the Sierra del Pozo area is dotted with various viewpoints, or 'Miradores', such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt;, Mirador de Peña Quesada, and Mirador de la Alcantarilla. These offer breathtaking vistas of the surrounding landscapes, including the Embalse de la Bolera and the Guadalentín valley, perfect for a scenic break during your run.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in the Pozo Alcón area?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for each route are not always available, many natural trails in the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the terrain and distance.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Pozo Alcón?
A: For families, the easier and shorter routes are generally recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Mirador Guazalamanco&lt;/a&gt; is a good option due to its shorter distance and scenic appeal, making it enjoyable for various ages.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Pozo Alcón are circular, offering a convenient way to explore the landscape without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989183384/enchanted-forest-of-las-higueras-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.0 km) circular trail.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Pozo Alcón?
A: The terrain around Pozo Alcón is highly varied, reflecting its location at the entrance to the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park. You can expect lush mountains, dense forests, riverside paths along the Guadalentín River, and trails around the tranquil Embalse de La Bolera. Surfaces can range from well-trodden paths to more rugged, mountainous sections.

Q: Are there any permits required for running in the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for casual running on established trails within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. However, for specific activities or access to certain restricted areas, it's always advisable to check with the park authorities for any current regulations.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about the trails in Pozo Alcón?
A: Runners often praise the diverse and stunning natural environment of Pozo Alcón. The combination of lush mountains, unique features like the 'Enchanted Forest,' and the serene waters of the Embalse de La Bolera provides a captivating backdrop for any run. The variety of terrain also allows for different running preferences and fitness levels.
tips: Set of small waterfalls that form the Guazalamanco waterfalls, located on the river of the same name. After a rainy season, the waterfalls offer a very beautiful natural spectacle.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain. You'll find wide, well-defined paths suitable for steady jogging, but also narrower, more wooded sections. Be prepared for strong descents and ascents, and some stony or rocky ground, especially in the Barranco de las Sabinas area. You'll also need to cross the Guazalamanco stream and navigate some streambed sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the impressive Guazalamanco Waterfall, especially after rain. The trail also offers captivating natural beauty with well-preserved forests, diverse flora, and glimpses of the Bolera reservoir. In summer, some pools along the Guazalamanco stream are even suitable for swimming.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: While some sections are easy to walk and accessible, the overall route is rated as difficult for jogging. It requires very good fitness and some sure-footedness due to elevation changes, rocky surfaces, and narrower paths. It might be challenging for beginner joggers to maintain a continuous run throughout.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. Specific parking details for the Barranco de las Sabinas or Guazalamanco sections are not provided, but generally, trailheads in natural parks have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Barranco de las Sabinas to Guazalamanco Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information specific to dogs on this exact trail is not available. However, as it's located within a Natural Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always follow park regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but the waterfalls are most impressive after periods of rain. Summer offers the added bonus of pools suitable for swimming. Consider cooler months for jogging to avoid intense heat, especially given the trail's difficulty.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park itself. However, certain activities or specific areas within the park might require permits or have restrictions. For jogging this specific route, a general permit is typically not needed, but it's always good to check the official park website for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any natural water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no natural water sources available along the entire route. It is crucial to carry sufficient hydration for your entire jog, especially considering the trail's length and difficulty.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route primarily follows the Barranco de las Sabinas a Guazalamanco trail. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Transandalus and the Sendero de los Furtivos.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and sometimes rocky terrain, wear sturdy trail running shoes with good grip. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and always carry sufficient water as there are no natural sources. Sun protection, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit are also recommended.

faqs.question: Can I swim in any of the pools along the Guazalamanco stream?
faqs.answer: Yes, in summer, some of the pools created by the Guazalamanco stream are suitable for swimming. This can be a refreshing reward after your jog, especially on a warm day.
